sanskrit: वायुर्यमोऽग्निर्वरुणः शशाङ्कःप्रजापतिस्त्वं प्रपितामहश्च । नमो नमस्तेऽस्तु सहस्रकृत्वःपुनश्च भूयोऽपि नमो नमस्ते ॥
hindiTranslation: आप ही वायु, यमराज, अग्नि, वरुण, चन्द्रमा, दक्ष आदि प्रजापति और प्रपितामह ब्रह्माजीके भी पिता हैं ।  आपको हजारों बार नमस्कार हो नमस्कार हो   और फिर भी आपको बारबार नमस्कार हो  नमस्कार हो ॥
englishTranslation: You are air, Yama, fire, Varuna, the moon, Prajapati, and the great ancestor. I bow to you a thousand times, and again and again.
essence: All powers and forces bow inside the one you face.
speaker: Arjuna
simpleMeaning: Arjuna sees Krishna as the source behind all cosmic powers and offers repeated bowing. The response is not argument but reverence.
simpleInsight: Awe can dissolve pride faster than explanation can.
detailedMeaning: On the Kurukshetra battlefield, Arjuna is overwhelmed by Krishna's cosmic form. After seeing the vast power behind the universe, he names the forces of nature and creation as Krishna's own presence, then bows again and again in reverence.
modernRelevance: You stand before something too large to control — a diagnosis, a confession, a loss you cannot undo. The mind stops bargaining and simply bows.
translationLiteral: वायुः (air) / यमः (Yama) / अग्निः (fire) / वरुणः (Varuna) / शशाङ्कः (moon) / प्रजापतिः (Prajapati) / त्वम् (you) / प्रपितामहः (great ancestor) / च (and) / नमः नमः ते (salutations, salutations to you) / अस्तु (may it be) / सहस्रकृत्वः (a thousand times) / पुनः (again) / च (and) / भूयः अपि (again and again) / नमः नमः ते (salutations, salutations to you)
